title: [Bug]: Oracle benchmark reports message rates for non-message phases
summary: The sanitized Oracle benchmark report calculates messages-per-second for every phase from the configured message count. Retry-delay and shutdown phases are timing or lifecycle observations rather than message-processing throughput phases, so reporting message rates for them can mislead operators.
observed: When retry-delay or shutdown timing observations are present, the benchmark renderer can publish messages-per-second values based on the full benchmark message count.
expected: Message-processing phases such as publish, fetch, map, write, commit, and ACK may report messages-per-second. Retry-delay and shutdown phases should report timing only unless a future implementation records explicit phase-specific work counts.
reproduction: Build an Oracle benchmark report with retry and shutdown observations and inspect the rendered phase dictionaries for messages_per_second on those non-message phases.
failing_test: Add a focused regression test under tests/unit that proves retry and shutdown benchmark phases omit messages_per_second.
impact: This is a low-severity reporting accuracy issue. It does not change live NATS, Oracle writes, ACK behavior, idempotency, DLQ behavior, or benchmark execution. It only corrects public-safe benchmark report interpretation.
delivery_semantics: No delivery semantics are changed. Oracle commit-before-ACK behavior remains untouched.
security: The fix must preserve benchmark redaction and must not add service endpoints, usernames, passwords, wallet paths, certificates, private subjects, table names, or payload bodies to benchmark output.
acceptance: A focused regression test covers retry and shutdown benchmark phase-rate output.
Message-processing phases still report messages_per_second when observations exist.
Oracle benchmark documentation explains which phases represent throughput and which phases are lifecycle timing observations.
Existing unit, smoke, documentation, package, and release checks still pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, Oracle benchmark unit tests, synthetic load-profile tests, smoke tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and docs/performance.md to explain the benchmark phase-rate distinction.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
